questions.question: At which layer of the OSI model does a standard IP router primarily operate?
What protocol is used to automatically assign IP addresses to devices on a network?
Which cryptographic primitive guarantees non-repudiation in digital communications?
What security attack exploits vulnerable software code by writing more data to a memory buffer than it can hold, overwriting adjacent memory?
In the TCP 3-way handshake, what sequence of flags is exchanged to establish a connection?
What is the purpose of the Address Resolution Protocol (ARP)?
answers: To resolve domain names to IP addresses
To map an IP address to a physical MAC address
To securely route packets across the internet
To manage session state in web applications
questions.correctAnswer: To map an IP address to a physical MAC address

questions.question: Which HTTP status code represents an internal server error?
What type of attack involves an attacker placing themselves between two communicating parties to secretly intercept or alter traffic?
Which asymmetric encryption algorithm relies on the mathematical difficulty of factoring large prime numbers?
What is the primary difference between IPv4 and IPv6 address sizes?
